skill.name: verification-before-completion
skill.description: Requires fresh verification evidence before claiming work is complete, fixed, passing, or ready for commit or pull request.
skill.summary: Enforces evidence-based completion claims before reporting success.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, assertive wording, and broad command guidance as potential security issues. Review found no executable scripts, no hardcoded commands, no network calls, and no prompt injection attempt. The skill is low risk because it encourages users to run local verification commands before making success claims.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misidentified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The static Ruby backtick detections point to Markdown code fences and examples, not executable Ruby or shell code. No script file or command interpolation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are Markdown fence delimiters around prose examples. There is no executable Ruby file, shell snippet, or user-controlled command expansion.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is Not Supported
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The high-severity cryptography finding points to the front matter description. It contains workflow guidance and no cryptographic algorithm or crypto operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 3 is natural-language metadata about verification before completion. I found no evidence of MD5, SHA-1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic implementation.

low_findings.title: Reconnaissance Findings Are Natural-Language Guidance
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The system and network reconnaissance detections point to ordinary process text. The skill does not enumerate hosts, ports, users, interfaces, or network targets.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines discuss independently verifying agent reports, trust, and wording implications. No commands or instructions for system or network discovery are present.

low_findings.title: General External Command Guidance Requires User Judgment
low_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The skill tells users to identify and run verification commands. This is legitimate for testing and builds, but users should only run trusted local commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command-running guidance is explicit and central to the skill. Risk remains low because no specific dangerous command, injection vector, or remote endpoint is supplied.

content.user_title: Verify Claims Before Marking Work Complete
content.value_statement: AI agents can overstate completion when tests or checks were not run. This skill requires fresh evidence before success claims, commits, or pull requests.
actual_capabilities: Requires fresh verification before any completion or success claim.
Defines a five-step gate for identifying, running, reading, and confirming checks.
Lists common claims and the evidence needed to support them.
Flags risky language such as should, probably, and seems to.
Promotes independent verification of delegated agent work.
limitations: Does not provide project-specific test, build, or lint commands.
Does not execute checks by itself or parse tool output automatically.
Cannot prove correctness beyond the verification commands selected by the user.
May add time to small changes that still need evidence.

use_cases.title: Pre-Commit Verification
use_cases.description: Run the right checks and cite results before committing code changes.

use_cases.title: Pull Request Readiness
use_cases.description: Confirm tests, builds, and requirements before opening or updating a pull request.

use_cases.title: Delegated Work Review
use_cases.description: Verify another agent's reported success with diffs and independent checks.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Completion Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before saying a task is done.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply verification-before-completion. Identify the command that proves this task is complete, run it, read the output, and report only supported claims.

prompt_templates.title: Bug Fix Verification
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after changing code for a defect.
prompt_templates.prompt: Before claiming this bug is fixed, verify the original symptom. Run the relevant test or reproduction check and summarize the evidence.

prompt_templates.title: Pull Request Gate
prompt_templates.scenario: Use before creating or updating a pull request.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the verification gate before PR work. Check tests, build status, and requirements, then list any gaps before making readiness claims.

prompt_templates.title: Delegated Agent Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Use after another agent reports success.
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply verification-before-completion to the delegated result. Review the diff, verify the claimed changes, run fresh checks, and report the actual state.

output_examples.input: The model wants to say all tests pass after editing code.
output_examples.output: It runs the full test command, reads the final summary, then reports the exact pass count and any failures.

output_examples.input: A delegated agent says a feature is complete.
output_examples.output: It checks the version control diff, verifies requirements one by one, and reports confirmed work and unresolved gaps.

output_examples.input: A developer plans to open a pull request.
output_examples.output: It runs fresh build and test checks, confirms exit status, and only then states whether the pull request is ready.

best_practices: Choose the verification command before making any completion claim.
Read the full output and include the result that supports the claim.
Report gaps plainly when verification fails or is incomplete.
anti_patterns: Saying work should pass because the code looks correct.
Treating a partial check as proof that all requirements are met.
Trusting another agent's success report without independent verification.

faq.question: Does this skill run tests automatically?
faq.answer: No. It instructs the agent to choose and run appropriate verification commands in the current project.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it before claiming work is complete, fixed, passing, ready to commit, or ready for pull request review.

faq.question: Can it replace test coverage?
faq.answer: No. It improves reporting discipline, but it depends on the project's available tests and checks.

faq.question: Why is the risk level low?
faq.answer: The skill asks users to run local verification commands, but it contains no hardcoded dangerous commands or network calls.

faq.question: Does it support Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.

faq.question: What evidence should the agent report?
faq.answer: It should report the command run, the relevant output summary, the exit status, and any remaining failures or gaps.
